steps.title: Recall what standard deviation measures
steps.content: Standard deviation measures how spread out a distribution is. A distribution clustered tightly near its center has a small standard deviation; one with values spread far from the center has a large standard deviation.

steps.title: Compare the two histograms
steps.content:
Team A's histogram is concentrated near the center (most scores cluster around the middle bins).

Team B's histogram is more spread out, with significant counts in the extreme bins.

So team A has the smaller spread: $\sigma_A \lt \sigma_B$.

That confirms A is correct.
